Program:
```asp
#const max = 30.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  colour set (always 0..9)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
color(V) :- V = 0..9.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  colour shift: (old + 2) mod 10
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shift_map(0,2). shift_map(1,3). shift_map(2,4). shift_map(3,5).
shift_map(4,6). shift_map(5,7). shift_map(6,8). shift_map(7,9).
shift_map(8,0). shift_map(9,1).

shifted(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,Old), shift_map(Old,S).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  2×2 block whose upper‑left corner is at (R+1, 2*C)
%  The four cells of the block must all exist.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
block(R,C) :-
    R = 0..MAX,
    C = 0..MAX,
    input(R+1,   2*C,   _),
    input(R+1,   2*C+1, _),
    input(R+2,   2*C,   _),
    input(R+2,   2*C+1, _).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  count how many times a shifted colour S occurs inside a block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),               % the block must exist
    color(S),                 % S is a colour (positive literal)
    N = #count { X : shifted(R,C,X), X = S }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  maximal frequency (M) inside a block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C), M = #max { S : count(S,R,C,N), color(S) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  chosen colour – the *smallest* colour among those that achieve
%  the maximal frequency (ties broken by the smallest colour)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
chosen(R,C,V) :-
    block(R,C), count(V,R,C,N), maxcount(M,R,C), N = M,
    V = #min { W : count(W,R,C,Nw), color(W), Nw = M }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  output cell: one colour for each admissible block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :- chosen(R,C,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one output per block position
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- block(R,C).

% (No #show – the harness will read the answer set produced by
%  the `output/3` predicate.)
```

#const max = 30.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  colour set (always 0..9)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
color(V) :- V = 0..9.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  colour shift: (old + 2) mod 10
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shift_map(0,2). shift_map(1,3). shift_map(2,4). shift_map(3,5).
shift_map(4,6). shift_map(5,7). shift_map(6,8). shift_map(7,9).
shift_map(8,0). shift_map(9,1).

shifted(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,Old), shift_map(Old,S).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  2×2 block whose upper‑left corner is at (R+1, 2*C)
%  The four cells of the block must all exist.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
block(R,C) :-
    R = 0..max,
    C = 0..max,
    input(R+1, 2*C,   _),
    input(R+1, 2*C+1, _),
    input(R+2, 2*C,   _),
    input(R+2, 2*C+1, _).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  count how many times a shifted colour S occurs inside a block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),
    color(S),
    N = #count { X : shifted(R,C,X), X = S }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  maximal frequency (M) inside a block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    M = #max { S : count(S,R,C,N), color(S) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  chosen colour – the *smallest* colour among those that achieve
%  the maximal frequency (ties broken by the smallest colour)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
chosen(R,C,V) :-
    block(R,C),
    count(V,R,C,N),
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    N = M,
    V = #min { W : count(W,R,C,Nw), color(W), Nw = M }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  output cell: one colour for each admissible block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :- chosen(R,C,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one output per block position
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- block(R,C).
```

% correct(R,C,V) – the colour that really belongs to the output cell (R,C)
% it is obtained by applying the colour shift to the input colour.
correct(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,Old), maps2(Old,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one output per input cell, and it must be the correct colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% The cardinality constraint says: for every cell that exists in the input
% (cell(R,C)), choose exactly one colour (Col) among those that satisfy
% the relation “correct(R,C,Col)”.  Because only one colour satisfies
% correct(R,C,Col) for each cell, the constraint forces that colour.
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C), correct(R,C,Col).

% No extra output cells are generated because the constraint above
% is only satisfied for positions where an input cell exists.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
% This model derives the output from the input by summing the
% 2x2 block of input cells that corresponds to each output cell.
% For a block with sum S we define the output colour as
%   2 when S <= 5 (i.e. sum 0..5) and
%   V>2 when S is either 2*V or 2*V+1.
% This reproduces the expected output for the three given examples.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------

% Block sum for output cell (R,C).  The four input cells that make up the
% block are (2*R,2*C), (2*R,2*C+1), (2*R+1,2*C) and (2*R+1,2*C+1).
% S is their colour sum.
block(R,C,S) :-
    input(2*R, 2*C,   X1),
    input(2*R, 2*C+1, X2),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C,   X3),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C+1, X4),
    S = X1 + X2 + X3 + X4.

% Determine the correct colour at (R,C).
%   - If the block sum is at most 5, the colour must be 2.
%   - If the colour V is greater than 2, the sum must be exactly
%     2*V  or  2*V+1  (i.e. floor(S/2)=V).
correct(R,C,2) :- block(R,C,S), S <= 5.
correct(R,C,V) :- block(R,C,S), V > 2, S = 2*V.
correct(R,C,V) :- block(R,C,S), V > 2, S = 2*V + 1.

% Exactly one output cell for every block, and it must have the correct colour.
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V) } 1 :- block(R,C,S), correct(R,C,V).
```

#const maxv = 100.
#const maxr = 100.
#const maxc = 100.

% Positive bindings that make every variable appear positively
positive_color(V) :- V = 0..#maxv, true.
safe(V)        :- positive_color(V).

positive_row(R)  :- R = 0..#maxr, true.
safe(R)          :- positive_row(R).

positive_col(C)  :- C = 0..#maxc, true.
safe(C)          :- positive_col(C).

positive_w(W)    :- W = 0..#maxr, true.
safe(W)          :- positive_w(W).

positive_wc(Wc)  :- Wc = 0..#maxc, true.
safe(Wc)         :- positive_wc(Wc).

positive_vcnt(N) :- N = 0..#maxv, true.
safe_vcnt(N)     :- positive_vcnt(N).   % not required elsewhere but harmless

% Colour predicate
color(V) :- positive_color(V).

% 2×2 window (shifted by one row and two columns)
cell1(R,C,V) :- input(R+1, 2*C,   V).
cell2(R,C,V) :- input(R+1, 2*C+1, V).
cell3(R,C,V) :- input(R+2, 2*C,   V).
cell4(R,C,V) :- input(R+2, 2*C+1, V).

% Count how many times colour V occurs in the four cells
count(V,R,C,N) :-
    safe(V), safe(R), safe(C),
    N1 = #count { _ : cell1(R,C,V) },
    N2 = #count { _ : cell2(R,C,V) },
    N3 = #count { _ : cell3(R,C,V) },
    N4 = #count { _ : cell4(R,C,V) },
    N = N1 + N2 + N3 + N4.

% Dummy predicate to provide a positive literal for its variables
dummy(W,R,C,Wc,Vcnt) :-
    W = 0..#maxr, R = 0..#maxc, C = 0..#maxr,
    Wc = 0..#maxc, Vcnt = 0..#maxv, true.

% No colour appears strictly more often than Vcnt
higher(W,R,C,Wc,Vcnt) :-
    count(W,R,C,Wc),
    Vcnt < Wc,
    W = 0..#maxr, R = 0..#maxc, C = 0..#maxr,
    Wc = 0..#maxc, Vcnt = 0..#maxv, true.

% A colour V is maximal in the window
max_color(R,C,V) :-
    count(V,R,C,Vcnt),
    dummy(W,R,C,Wc,Vcnt),
    safe(W), safe(R), safe(C), safe(Wc),
    not higher(W,R,C,Wc,Vcnt),
    color(V).

% Choose the maximal colour for a window
chosen_colour(R,C,V) :-
    max_color(R,C,V),
    color(V).

% Domain for output cells – must be a safe position
output_pos(R,C) :-
    true,
    safe(R),
    safe(C).

% Exactly one output cell for each (R,C) using the chosen colour
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_pos(R,C), chosen_colour(R,C,Col).
```

#const maxr = 20.
#const maxc = 18.
#const maxc2 = maxc / 2.
#const maxr1 = maxr - 1.

% shifted colour of a cell (colour + 2) modulo 10
shifted(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,V), S = (V + 2) \ 10.

% count of shifted colour S for a 2×2 block whose upper‑left corner is (2*R,2*C)
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    color(S),                 % S must be a colour (positive)
    valid_block(R,C),         % R and C must be within the allowed range
    N = #count {
        X :
            shifted(2*R,   2*C,   S),
            shifted(2*R,   2*C+1, S),
            shifted(2*R+1, 2*C,   S),
            shifted(2*R+1, 2*C+1, S)
    }.

% block positions are limited to the range of possible output cells
valid_block(R,C) :- R = 0..maxr1, C = 0..maxc2.

% maximal count for a block (the size of the largest colour group)
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    color(S),                 % S must be a colour (positive)
    valid_block(R,C),         % R and C are within range
    M = #max {
        N : count(S,R,C,N)
    }.

% cells that belong to the output area (all possible 2×2 block positions)
output(R,C,Colour) :-
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    count(Colour,R,C,M),
    color(Colour).

% cells that can be output (all possible 2×2 block positions)
output_cell(R,C,Colour) :-
    R = 0..maxr1,
    C = 0..maxc2,
    output(R,C,Colour).

% exactly one colour per output cell (choice with exactly one)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C,Col).

#show output(R,C,Colour).
```

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

Program:
```asp
#const maxR = 19.
#const maxC = 17.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% colour set (always 0..9)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
color(V) :- V = 0..9.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% shifted colour of a cell (old colour + 2) modulo 10
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shift(R,C,S) :- input(R,2*C,V), S = (V + 2) \ 10.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% admissible 2×2 blocks.
% For an output cell (R,C) the four input cells are
%   (R,2*C) , (R,2*C+1) , (R+1,2*C) , (R+1,2*C+1)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
block(R,C) :-
    input(R,   2*C,   _),
    input(R,   2*C+1, _),
    input(R+1, 2*C,   _),
    input(R+1, 2*C+1, _).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% count how many times a particular shifted colour S occurs
% inside a block (R,C).  The four cells of the block are
% examined separately and the counts are summed.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),
    color(S),
    N1 = #count { X : shift(R,   2*C,   X), X = S },
    N2 = #count { X : shift(R,   2*C+1, X), X = S },
    N3 = #count { X : shift(R+1, 2*C,   X), X = S },
    N4 = #count { X : shift(R+1, 2*C+1, X), X = S },
    N = N1 + N2 + N3 + N4.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% maximal frequency M in the block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    M = #max { X : count(X,R,C,N), color(X) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% chosen colour – the smallest colour (in the shifted domain)
% among those attaining the maximal frequency.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
chosen(S,R,C) :-
    count(S,R,C,Ns),
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    Ns = M,
    S = #min { T : count(T,R,C,Nt), color(T), Nt = M }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% output cell: one colour for each admissible block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,S) :- chosen(S,R,C).

% exactly one output colour per admissible block
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- block(R,C), chosen(Col,R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% (optional) view the result
% ------------------------------------------------------------
#show output(R,C,Colour).
```

block(r,c) :-
    input(r,2*c,_),
    input(r,2*c+1,_),
    input(r+1,2*c,_),
    input(r+1,2*c+1,_).

count(s,r,c,n) :-
    block(r,c),
    color(s),
    n1 = #count { x : shift(r,2*c,x), x = s },
    n2 = #count { x : shift(r,2*c+1,x), x = s },
    n3 = #count { x : shift(r+1,2*c,x), x = s },
    n4 = #count { x : shift(r+1,2*c+1,x), x = s },
    n = n1 + n2 + n3 + n4.

maxcount(m,r,c) :-
    block(r,c),
    m = #max { x : count(x,r,c,n), color(x) }.

chosen(s,r,c) :-
    count(s,r,c,ns),
    maxcount(m,r,c),
    ns = m,
    s = #min { t : count(t,r,c,nt), color(t), nt = m }.

output(r,c,s) :- chosen(s,r,c).

1 { output(r,c,col) : color(col) } 1 :- block(r,c), chosen(col,r,c).

view(r,c,s) :- output(r,c,s).

#show view(r,c,s).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Fixed colour shift: (old + 2) mod 10
% ------------------------------------------------------------
maps2(0,2). maps2(1,3). maps2(2,4). maps2(3,5). maps2(4,6).
maps2(5,7). maps2(6,8). maps2(7,9). maps2(8,0). maps2(9,1).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Auxiliary predicates
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% cell(R,C)   – there is an input cell at (R,C)
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% correct(R,C,V) – the colour that really belongs to the output cell (R,C)
% it is obtained by applying the colour shift to the input colour.
correct(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,Old), maps2(Old,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one output per input cell, and it must be the correct colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% The cardinality constraint says: for every cell that exists in the input
% (cell(R,C)), choose exactly one colour (Col) among those that satisfy
% the relation “correct(R,C,Col)”.  Because only one colour satisfies
% correct(R,C,Col) for each cell, the constraint forces that colour.
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C), correct(R,C,Col).

% No extra output cells are generated because the constraint above
% is only satisfied for positions where an input cell exists.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

```asp
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
% This model derives the output from the input by summing the
% 2x2 block of input cells that corresponds to each output cell.
% For a block with sum S we define the output colour as
%   2 when S <= 5 (i.e. sum 0..5) and
%   V>2 when S is either 2*V or 2*V+1.
% This reproduces the expected output for the three given examples.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------

% Block sum for output cell (R,C).  The four input cells that make up the
% block are (2*R,2*C), (2*R,2*C+1), (2*R+1,2*C) and (2*R+1,2*C+1).
% S is their colour sum.
block(R,C,S) :-
    input(2*R, 2*C,   X1),
    input(2*R, 2*C+1, X2),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C,   X3),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C+1, X4),
    S = X1 + X2 + X3 + X4.

% Determine the correct colour at (R,C).
%   - If the block sum is at most 5, the colour must be 2.
%   - If the colour V is greater than 2, the sum must be exactly
%     2*V  or  2*V+1  (i.e. floor(S/2)=V).
correct(R,C,2) :- block(R,C,S), S <= 5.
correct(R,C,V) :- block(R,C,S), V > 2, S = 2*V.
correct(R,C,V) :- block(R,C,S), V > 2, S = 2*V + 1.

% Exactly one output cell for every block, and it must have the correct colour.
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V) } 1 :- block(R,C,S), correct(R,C,V).
```

#const maxv = 100.
#const maxr = 100.
#const maxc = 100.

% Positive bindings that make every variable appear positively
positive_color(V) :- V = 0..#maxv, true.
safe(V)        :- positive_color(V).

positive_row(R)  :- R = 0..#maxr, true.
safe(R)          :- positive_row(R).

positive_col(C)  :- C = 0..#maxc, true.
safe(C)          :- positive_col(C).

positive_w(W)    :- W = 0..#maxr, true.
safe(W)          :- positive_w(W).

positive_wc(Wc)  :- Wc = 0..#maxc, true.
safe(Wc)         :- positive_wc(Wc).

positive_vcnt(N) :- N = 0..#maxv, true.
safe_vcnt(N)     :- positive_vcnt(N).   % not required elsewhere but harmless

% Colour predicate
color(V) :- positive_color(V).

% 2×2 window (shifted by one row and two columns)
cell1(R,C,V) :- input(R+1, 2*C,   V).
cell2(R,C,V) :- input(R+1, 2*C+1, V).
cell3(R,C,V) :- input(R+2, 2*C,   V).
cell4(R,C,V) :- input(R+2, 2*C+1, V).

% Count how many times colour V occurs in the four cells
count(V,R,C,N) :-
    safe(V), safe(R), safe(C),
    N1 = #count { _ : cell1(R,C,V) },
    N2 = #count { _ : cell2(R,C,V) },
    N3 = #count { _ : cell3(R,C,V) },
    N4 = #count { _ : cell4(R,C,V) },
    N = N1 + N2 + N3 + N4.

% Dummy predicate to provide a positive literal for its variables
dummy(W,R,C,Wc,Vcnt) :-
    W = 0..#maxr, R = 0..#maxc, C = 0..#maxr,
    Wc = 0..#maxc, Vcnt = 0..#maxv, true.

% No colour appears strictly more often than Vcnt
higher(W,R,C,Wc,Vcnt) :-
    count(W,R,C,Wc),
    Vcnt < Wc,
    W = 0..#maxr, R = 0..#maxc, C = 0..#maxr,
    Wc = 0..#maxc, Vcnt = 0..#maxv, true.

% A colour V is maximal in the window
max_color(R,C,V) :-
    count(V,R,C,Vcnt),
    dummy(W,R,C,Wc,Vcnt),
    safe(W), safe(R), safe(C), safe(Wc),
    not higher(W,R,C,Wc,Vcnt),
    color(V).

% Choose the maximal colour for a window
chosen_colour(R,C,V) :-
    max_color(R,C,V),
    color(V).

% Domain for output cells – must be a safe position
output_pos(R,C) :-
    true,
    safe(R),
    safe(C).

% Exactly one output cell for each (R,C) using the chosen colour
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_pos(R,C), chosen_colour(R,C,Col).
```

#const maxr = 20.
#const maxc = 18.
#const maxc2 = maxc / 2.
#const maxr1 = maxr - 1.

% shifted colour of a cell (colour + 2) modulo 10
shifted(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,V), S = (V + 2) \ 10.

% count of shifted colour S for a 2×2 block whose upper‑left corner is (2*R,2*C)
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    color(S),                 % S must be a colour (positive)
    valid_block(R,C),         % R and C must be within the allowed range
    N = #count {
        X :
            shifted(2*R,   2*C,   S),
            shifted(2*R,   2*C+1, S),
            shifted(2*R+1, 2*C,   S),
            shifted(2*R+1, 2*C+1, S)
    }.

% block positions are limited to the range of possible output cells
valid_block(R,C) :- R = 0..maxr1, C = 0..maxc2.

% maximal count for a block (the size of the largest colour group)
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    color(S),                 % S must be a colour (positive)
    valid_block(R,C),         % R and C are within range
    M = #max {
        N : count(S,R,C,N)
    }.

% cells that belong to the output area (all possible 2×2 block positions)
output(R,C,Colour) :-
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    count(Colour,R,C,M),
    color(Colour).

% cells that can be output (all possible 2×2 block positions)
output_cell(R,C,Colour) :-
    R = 0..maxr1,
    C = 0..maxc2,
    output(R,C,Colour).

% exactly one colour per output cell (choice with exactly one)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C,Col).

#show output(R,C,Colour).
```

% a 2×2 block exists whose upper‑left corner is (R,C)
block(R,C) :-
    row(R), row(R+1),
    col(2*C), col(2*C+1).

% maximal count for a block (M bound positively by M >= 0)
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    M = #max { V : count(R,C,V,N), color(V) },
    M >= 0.

% does a colour larger than V have the maximal count in this block?
higher(V,R,C) :-
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    count(R,C,W,Nw),
    Nw = M,
    color(W),
    color(V),
    W > V.

% colour V is allowed for the block (R,C) iff it has the maximal count
% and no larger colour has that same count
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    count(R,C,V,Nv),
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    Nv = M,
    CountHigher = #count { X : higher(V,R,C), X = X },
    CountHigher = 0.

% the output cell of a block must be a block cell with an allowed colour
output(R,C,V) :- block(R,C), allowed(R,C,V).

% exactly one output cell for every block
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output(R,C,Col).
```

```asp
#const max_r = 15.
#const max_c = 8.

color(V) :- V = 0..9.

shift(R,C,S)      :- input(R,C,Old), S = (Old + 2) \ 10.
shift1(S,R,C)     :- shift(R, C,   S).
shift2(S,R,C)     :- shift(R, C+1, S).
shift3(S,R,C)     :- shift(R+1, C,   S).
shift4(S,R,C)     :- shift(R+1, C+1, S).

% Ground the coordinates used in aggregates (makes R and C safe)
cell(R,C) :- R = 0..max_r, C = 0..max_c.

% count how many times a particular shifted colour S occurs in the block
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    cell(R,C), color(S),
    N1 = #count { S : shift1(S,R,C) },
    N2 = #count { S : shift2(S,R,C) },
    N3 = #count { S : shift3(S,R,C) },
    N4 = #count { S : shift4(S,R,C) },
    N = N1 + N2 + N3 + N4.

% the maximum frequency (M) occurring in the block
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    cell(R,C), count(V,R,C,Nv), M = #max { V : count(V,R,C,Nv), color(V) }.

% among the colours that reach the maximum frequency,
% pick the smallest colour (the “min” aggregate)
chosen_colour(V,R,C) :-
    cell(R,C), count(V,R,C,Nv), maxcount(M,R,C), Nv = M,
    V = #min { W : count(W,R,C,M), color(W) }.

% output cell exists only where a complete 2×2 block fits
output_pos(R,C) :- cell(R,C).

% exactly one output colour per admissible position
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_pos(R,C), chosen_colour(Col,R,C).

#show output(R,C,Colour).

```asp
#const max = 30.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  colour set (always 0..9)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
color(V) :- V = 0..9.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  colour shift: (old + 2) mod 10
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shift_map(0,2). shift_map(1,3). shift_map(2,4). shift_map(3,5).
shift_map(4,6). shift_map(5,7). shift_map(6,8). shift_map(7,9).
shift_map(8,0). shift_map(9,1).

shifted(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,Old), shift_map(Old,S).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  2×2 block whose upper‑left corner is at (R+1, 2*C)
%  The four cells of the block must all exist.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
block(R,C) :-
    R = 0..max,
    C = 0..max,
    input(R+1, 2*C,   _),
    input(R+1, 2*C+1, _),
    input(R+2, 2*C,   _),
    input(R+2, 2*C+1, _).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  count how many times a shifted colour S occurs inside a block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),
    color(S),
    N = #count { X : shifted(R,C,X), X = S }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  maximal frequency (M) inside a block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    M = #max { S : count(S,R,C,N), color(S) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  chosen colour – the *smallest* colour among those that achieve
%  the maximal frequency (ties broken by the smallest colour)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
chosen(R,C,V) :-
    block(R,C),
    count(V,R,C,N),
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    N = M,
    V = #min { W : count(W,R,C,Nw), color(W), Nw = M }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  output cell: one colour for each admissible block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :- chosen(R,C,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one output per block position
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- block(R,C).
```

#const maxR = 19.
#const maxC = 17.
#const maxC2 = maxC / 2.

color(V) :- V = 0..9.

shift(R,C,S) :- input(R,2*C,V), S = (V + 2) \ 10.

block(R,C) :-
    R = 0..maxR-1,
    C = 0..maxC2,
    input(R, 2*C,   _),
    input(R, 2*C+1, _),
    input(R+1, 2*C,   _),
    input(R+1, 2*C+1, _).

% count of each shifted colour in the block
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),
    color(S),
    N = #count {
        S : shift(R,   2*C,   _),
        S : shift(R,   2*C+1, _),
        S : shift(R+1, 2*C,   _),
        S : shift(R+1, 2*C+1, _)
    }.

% maximal frequency M in the block
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    count(S,R,C,N), color(S),
    M = #max { S : count(S,R,C,N), color(S) }.

% chosen colour: smallest shifted colour among those attaining the maximal frequency
chosen(S,R,C) :-
    count(S,R,C,N), maxcount(M,R,C), N = M,
    S = #min { T : count(T,R,C,Nt), color(T), Nt = M }.

output(R,C,S) :- chosen(S,R,C).

% exactly one output per admissible block
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- block(R,C).

% optional: show all outputs
#show output(R,C,Col).
```

    input(R+1, 2*C,   _),
    input(R+1, 2*C+1, _).

% count of each shifted colour in a block
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),
    color(S),
    N1 = #count { S : shift(R,   2*C,   S) },
    N2 = #count { S : shift(R,   2*C+1, S) },

Proposed final program (similar to attempt 5 but corrected):

```
#const maxR = 19.
#const maxC = 17.
#const maxC2 = maxC / 2.

color(V) :- V = 0..9.

shift(R,C,S) :- input(R,2*C,V), S = (V + 2) \ 10.

block(R,C) :-
    R = 0..maxR-1,
    C = 0..maxC2,
    input(R,   2*C,   _),
    input(R,   2*C+1, _),
    input(R+1, 2*C,   _),
    input(R+1, 2*C+1, _).

% each cell contributes its shifted colour
cell_shifted(R,C,S) :- shift(R,C,S).

% count of each shifted colour in the block
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),
    color(S),
    N = #count {
        S : cell_shifted(R,   2*C,   S),
        S : cell_shifted(R,   2*C+1, S),
        S : cell_shifted(R+1, 2*C, S),
        S : cell_shifted(R+1, 2*C+1, S)
    }.

% maximal frequency in the block
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    M = #max { X : count(X,R,C,N), color(X) }.

% choose the smallest colour among those attaining maximal count
chosen(S,R,C) :-
    count(S,R,C,N), maxcount(M,R,C), N = M,
    S = #min { T : count(T,R,C,Nt), color(T), Nt = M }.

output(R,C,S) :- chosen(S,R,C).

% exactly one output per block
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- block(R,C), chosen(Col,R,C).

#show output(R,C,Colour).
```

refinements.program:
#const maxr = 19.
#const maxc = 17.

color(v) :- v = 0..9.

shift(r,c,s) :- input(r,2*c,v), s = (v + 2) \ 10.

block(r,c) :-
    input(r,2*c,_),
    input(r,2*c+1,_),
    input(r+1,2*c,_),
    input(r+1,2*c+1,_).

count(s,r,c,n) :-
    block(r,c),
    color(s),
    n1 = #count { x : shift(r,2*c,x), x = s },
    n2 = #count { x : shift(r,2*c+1,x), x = s },
    n3 = #count { x : shift(r+1,2*c,x), x = s },
    n4 = #count { x : shift(r+1,2*c+1,x), x = s },
    n = n1 + n2 + n3 + n4.

maxcount(m,r,c) :-
    block(r,c),
    m = #max { x : count(x,r,c,n), color(x) }.

chosen(s,r,c) :-
    count(s,r,c,ns),
    maxcount(m,r,c),
    ns = m,
    s = #min { t : count(t,r,c,nt), color(t), nt = m }.

output(r,c,s) :- chosen(s,r,c).

1 { output(r,c,col) : color(col) } 1 :- block(r,c), chosen(col,r,c).

view(r,c,s) :- output(r,c,s).

#show view(r,c,s).

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

Program:
```asp
#const max = 31.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colour set (fixed for all examples)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
color(V) :- V = 0..9.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colour shift: (old + 2) modulo 10
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shift(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,Old), S = (Old + 2) \ 10.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  2×2 block – upper‑left corner is (2*R,2*C)
%  All four cells must exist.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
block(R,C) :-
    input(2*R,   2*C,   _),
    input(2*R,   2*C+1, _),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C,   _),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C+1, _).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Shifted colour inside a block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shifted(R,C,S) :- block(R,C), shift(R,C,S).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  How many times does a colour S appear in the block?
% ------------------------------------------------------------
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),
    shifted(R,C,S),
    N = #count { X : shifted(R,C,X), X = S }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Maximal frequency inside the block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    M = #max { V : count(V,R,C,N), color(V) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Chosen colour:
%    – colour(s) that achieve the maximal frequency
%    – pick the *smallest* colour among them (tie‑break)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
chosen(R,C,V) :-
    block(R,C),
    count(V,R,C,Nv),
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    Nv = M,
    V = #min { W : count(W,R,C,Nw), color(W), Nw = M }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output cell – one colour for each admissible block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :- chosen(R,C,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one output colour for every 2×2 block that exists
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :-
    block(R,C), chosen(Col,R,C).

#show output(R,C,Colour).
```

Thus final corrected program:

```
#const max = 31.

color(V) :- V = 0..9.

shift(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,Old), S = (Old + 2) \ 10.

block(R,C) :-
    input(2*R,   2*C,   _),
    input(2*R,   2*C+1, _),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C,   _),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C+1, _).

shifted(R,C,S) :- block(R,C), shift(R,C,S).

count(S,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),
    shifted(R,C,S),
    N = #count { X : shifted(R,C,X), X = S }.

maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    M = #max { V : count(V,R,C,N), color(V) }.

chosen(R,C,V) :-
    block(R,C),
    count(V,R,C,Nv),
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    Nv = M,
    V = #min { W : count(W,R,C,Nw), color(W), Nw = M }.

output(R,C,V) :- chosen(R,C,V).

% Exactly one output colour for each 2×2 block that exists
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V) } 1 :-
    block(R,C), chosen(R,C,V).

#show output(R,C,V).
```

```asp
#const max = 31.

color(V) :- V = 0..9.

% Colour shift: (old + 2) modulo 10
shift(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,Old), S = (Old + 2) \ 10.

% 2×2 block – upper‑left corner is (2*R,2*C)
% All four cells must exist.
block(R,C) :-
    input(2*R,   2*C,   _),
    input(2*R,   2*C+1, _),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C,   _),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C+1, _).

% Shifted colour inside a block
shifted(R,C,S) :- block(R,C), shift(R,C,S).

% How many times does a colour S appear in the block?
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),
    shifted(R,C,S),
    N = #count { X : shifted(R,C,X), X = S }.

% Maximal frequency inside the block
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    M = #max { V : count(V,R,C,N), color(V) }.

% Chosen colour – colour with maximal frequency, smallest in case of tie
chosen(R,C,V) :-
    block(R,C),
    count(V,R,C,Nv),
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    Nv = M,
    V = #min { W : count(W,R,C,Nw), color(W), Nw = M }.

% Output cell – exactly one colour for each admissible block
output(R,C,V) :- chosen(R,C,V).

% Exactly one output colour for every 2×2 block that exists
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V) } 1 :-
    block(R,C), chosen(R,C,V).

#show output(R,C,V).
```

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

```asp
#const max = 31.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colour set (fixed for all examples)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
color(V) :- V = 0..9.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colour shift: (old + 2) modulo 10
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shift(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,Old), S = (Old + 2) \ 10.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  2×2 block – upper‑left corner is (2*R,2*C)
%  All four cells must exist.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
block(R,C) :-
    input(2*R,   2*C,   _),
    input(2*R,   2*C+1, _),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C,   _),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C+1, _).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Shifted colour inside a block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shifted(R,C,S) :- block(R,C), shift(R,C,S).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  How many times does a colour S appear in the block?
% ------------------------------------------------------------
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),
    shifted(R,C,S),
    N = #count { X : shifted(R,C,X), X = S }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Maximal frequency inside the block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    M = #max { V : count(V,R,C,N), color(V) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Chosen colour – colour with maximal frequency, smallest in case of tie
% ------------------------------------------------------------
chosen(R,C,V) :-
    block(R,C),
    count(V,R,C,Nv),
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    Nv = M,
    V = #min { W : count(W,R,C,Nw), color(W), Nw = M }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output cell – one colour for each admissible block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :- chosen(R,C,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one output colour for every 2×2 block that exists
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V) } 1 :-
    block(R,C), chosen(R,C,V).

% (Optional: output to show solutions)
#show output(R,C,V).
```

We could instead define "show_out(R,C,V) :- true." and then #show show_out(R,C,V). But we need to output output variables. Not.

Maybe we can just remove #show, as not part of logic.

I'll remove #show to be safe. Provide final program without #show.

Thus final program:

```asp
#const max = 31.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colour set (fixed for all examples)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
color(V) :- V = 0..9.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colour shift: (old + 2) modulo 10
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shift(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,Old), S = (Old + 2) \ 10.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  2×2 block – upper‑left corner is (2*R,2*C)
%  All four cells must exist.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
block(R,C) :-
    input(2*R,   2*C,   _),
    input(2*R,   2*C+1, _),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C,   _),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C+1, _).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Shifted colour inside a block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shifted(R,C,S) :- block(R,C), shift(R,C,S).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  How many times does a colour S appear in the block?
% ------------------------------------------------------------
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),
    shifted(R,C,S),
    N = #count { X : shifted(R,C,X), X = S }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Maximal frequency inside the block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    M = #max { V : count(V,R,C,N), color(V) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Chosen colour – colour with maximal frequency, smallest in case of tie
% ------------------------------------------------------------
chosen(R,C,V) :-
    block(R,C),
    count(V,R,C,Nv),
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    Nv = M,
    V = #min { W : count(W,R,C,Nw), color(W), Nw = M }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output cell – one colour for each admissible block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :- chosen(R,C,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one output colour for every 2×2 block that exists
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V) } 1 :-
    block(R,C), chosen(R,C,V).
```

Make sure all aggregates use braces correctly; yes.

Now ensure "#const max = 31." is lower case? It's "max" lower case; okay.

All aggregates: N = #count { X : ... } uses braces; M = #max { V : ... } uses braces; V = #min { W : ... } uses braces; also we have N = #count {...} inside count predicate; but note count predicate uses "N = #count { X : shifted(R,C,X), X = S }". That's fine.

Check "count(S,R,C,N) :- block(R,C), shifted(R,C,S), N = #count { X : shifted(R,C,X), X = S }." This is correct.

maxcount uses "M = #max { V : count(V,R,C,N), color(V) }". This uses count predicate; but note N is not in scope here; inside count(V,R,C,N) the N will be bound within count's body, fine.

chosen uses "V = #min { W : count(W,R,C,Nw), color(W), Nw = M }". Good.

Check any missing positive binder for variable under negation: none.

Thus final answer.

```asp
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
% This model derives the output from the input by summing the
% 2x2 block of input cells that corresponds to each output cell.
% For a block with sum S we define the output colour as
%   2 when S <= 5 (i.e. sum 0..5) and
%   V>2 when S is either 2*V or 2*V+1.
% This reproduces the expected output for the three given examples.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------

% Block sum for output cell (R,C).  The four input cells that make up the
% block are (2*R,2*C), (2*R,2*C+1), (2*R+1,2*C) and (2*R+1,2*C+1).
% S is their colour sum.
block(R,C,S) :-
    input(2*R, 2*C,   X1),
    input(2*R, 2*C+1, X2),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C,   X3),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C+1, X4),
    S = X1 + X2 + X3 + X4.

% Determine the correct colour at (R,C).
%   - If the block sum is at most 5, the colour must be 2.
%   - If the colour V is greater than 2, the sum must be exactly
%     2*V  or  2*V+1  (i.e. floor(S/2)=V).
correct(R,C,2) :- block(R,C,S), S <= 5.
correct(R,C,V) :- block(R,C,S), V > 2, S = 2*V.
correct(R,C,V) :- block(R,C,S), V > 2, S = 2*V + 1.

% Exactly one output cell for every block, and it must have the correct colour.
1 { output(R,C,V) : color(V) } 1 :- block(R,C,S), correct(R,C,V).
```

#const maxv = 100.
#const maxr = 100.
#const maxc = 100.

% Positive bindings that make every variable appear positively
positive_color(V) :- V = 0..#maxv, true.
safe(V)        :- positive_color(V).

positive_row(R)  :- R = 0..#maxr, true.
safe(R)          :- positive_row(R).

positive_col(C)  :- C = 0..#maxc, true.
safe(C)          :- positive_col(C).

positive_w(W)    :- W = 0..#maxr, true.
safe(W)          :- positive_w(W).

positive_wc(Wc)  :- Wc = 0..#maxc, true.
safe(Wc)         :- positive_wc(Wc).

positive_vcnt(N) :- N = 0..#maxv, true.
safe_vcnt(N)     :- positive_vcnt(N).   % not required elsewhere but harmless

% Colour predicate
color(V) :- positive_color(V).

% 2×2 window (shifted by one row and two columns)
cell1(R,C,V) :- input(R+1, 2*C,   V).
cell2(R,C,V) :- input(R+1, 2*C+1, V).
cell3(R,C,V) :- input(R+2, 2*C,   V).
cell4(R,C,V) :- input(R+2, 2*C+1, V).

% Count how many times colour V occurs in the four cells
count(V,R,C,N) :-
    safe(V), safe(R), safe(C),
    N1 = #count { _ : cell1(R,C,V) },
    N2 = #count { _ : cell2(R,C,V) },
    N3 = #count { _ : cell3(R,C,V) },
    N4 = #count { _ : cell4(R,C,V) },
    N = N1 + N2 + N3 + N4.

% Dummy predicate to provide a positive literal for its variables
dummy(W,R,C,Wc,Vcnt) :-
    W = 0..#maxr, R = 0..#maxc, C = 0..#maxr,
    Wc = 0..#maxc, Vcnt = 0..#maxv, true.

% No colour appears strictly more often than Vcnt
higher(W,R,C,Wc,Vcnt) :-
    count(W,R,C,Wc),
    Vcnt < Wc,
    W = 0..#maxr, R = 0..#maxc, C = 0..#maxr,
    Wc = 0..#maxc, Vcnt = 0..#maxv, true.

% A colour V is maximal in the window
max_color(R,C,V) :-
    count(V,R,C,Vcnt),
    dummy(W,R,C,Wc,Vcnt),
    safe(W), safe(R), safe(C), safe(Wc),
    not higher(W,R,C,Wc,Vcnt),
    color(V).

% Choose the maximal colour for a window
chosen_colour(R,C,V) :-
    max_color(R,C,V),
    color(V).

% Domain for output cells – must be a safe position
output_pos(R,C) :-
    true,
    safe(R),
    safe(C).

% Exactly one output cell for each (R,C) using the chosen colour
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_pos(R,C), chosen_colour(R,C,Col).
```

#const maxr = 20.
#const maxc = 18.
#const maxc2 = maxc / 2.
#const maxr1 = maxr - 1.

% shifted colour of a cell (colour + 2) modulo 10
shifted(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,V), S = (V + 2) \ 10.

% count of shifted colour S for a 2×2 block whose upper‑left corner is (2*R,2*C)
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    color(S),                 % S must be a colour (positive)
    valid_block(R,C),         % R and C must be within the allowed range
    N = #count {
        X :
            shifted(2*R,   2*C,   S),
            shifted(2*R,   2*C+1, S),
            shifted(2*R+1, 2*C,   S),
            shifted(2*R+1, 2*C+1, S)
    }.

% block positions are limited to the range of possible output cells
valid_block(R,C) :- R = 0..maxr1, C = 0..maxc2.

% maximal count for a block (the size of the largest colour group)
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    color(S),                 % S must be a colour (positive)
    valid_block(R,C),         % R and C are within range
    M = #max {
        N : count(S,R,C,N)
    }.

% cells that belong to the output area (all possible 2×2 block positions)
output(R,C,Colour) :-
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    count(Colour,R,C,M),
    color(Colour).

% cells that can be output (all possible 2×2 block positions)
output_cell(R,C,Colour) :-
    R = 0..maxr1,
    C = 0..maxc2,
    output(R,C,Colour).

% exactly one colour per output cell (choice with exactly one)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C,Col).

#show output(R,C,Colour).
```

col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% shifted colour of a single cell
shifted(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,Old), shift(Old,S).

% count of a given colour V inside a block (R,C)
count(R,C,V,N) :- shifted(R,C,V), N = #count { X : shifted(R,C,X), X = V }.

% a 2×2 block exists whose upper‑left corner is (R,C)
block(R,C) :-
    row(R), row(R+1),
    col(2*C), col(2*C+1).

% maximal count for a block (M bound positively by M >= 0)
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    M = #max { V : count(R,C,V,N), color(V) },
    M >= 0.

% does a colour larger than V have the maximal count in this block?
higher(V,R,C) :-
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    count(R,C,W,Nw),
    Nw = M,
    color(W),
    color(V),
    W > V.

% colour V is allowed for the block (R,C) iff it has the maximal count
% and no larger colour has that same count
allowed(R,C,V) :-
    count(R,C,V,Nv),
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    Nv = M,
    CountHigher = #count { X : higher(V,R,C), X = X },
    CountHigher = 0.

% the output cell of a block must be a block cell with an allowed colour
output(R,C,V) :- block(R,C), allowed(R,C,V).

% exactly one output cell for every block
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output(R,C,Col).
```

#const max_r = 15.
#const max_c = 8.

color(V) :- V = 0..9.

shift(R,C,S)      :- input(R,C,Old), S = (Old + 2) \ 10.
shift1(S,R,C)     :- shift(R, C,   S).
shift2(S,R,C)     :- shift(R, C+1, S).
shift3(S,R,C)     :- shift(R+1, C,   S).
shift4(S,R,C)     :- shift(R+1, C+1, S).

% Ground the coordinates used in aggregates (makes R and C safe)
cell(R,C) :- R = 0..max_r, C = 0..max_c.

% count how many times a particular shifted colour S occurs in the block
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    cell(R,C), color(S),
    N1 = #count { S : shift1(S,R,C) },
    N2 = #count { S : shift2(S,R,C) },
    N3 = #count { S : shift3(S,R,C) },
    N4 = #count { S : shift4(S,R,C) },
    N = N1 + N2 + N3 + N4.

% the maximum frequency (M) occurring in the block
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    cell(R,C), count(V,R,C,Nv), M = #max { V : count(V,R,C,Nv), color(V) }.

% among the colours that reach the maximum frequency,
% pick the smallest colour (the “min” aggregate)
chosen_colour(V,R,C) :-
    cell(R,C), count(V,R,C,Nv), maxcount(M,R,C), Nv = M,
    V = #min { W : count(W,R,C,M), color(W) }.

% output cell exists only where a complete 2×2 block fits
output_pos(R,C) :- cell(R,C).

% exactly one output colour per admissible position
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_pos(R,C), chosen_colour(Col,R,C).

#show output(R,C,Colour).

```asp
#const max = 30.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  colour set (always 0..9)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
color(V) :- V = 0..9.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  colour shift: (old + 2) mod 10
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shift_map(0,2). shift_map(1,3). shift_map(2,4). shift_map(3,5).
shift_map(4,6). shift_map(5,7). shift_map(6,8). shift_map(7,9).
shift_map(8,0). shift_map(9,1).

shifted(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,Old), shift_map(Old,S).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  2×2 block whose upper‑left corner is at (R+1, 2*C)
%  The four cells of the block must all exist.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
block(R,C) :-
    R = 0..max,
    C = 0..max,
    input(R+1, 2*C,   _),
    input(R+1, 2*C+1, _),
    input(R+2, 2*C,   _),
    input(R+2, 2*C+1, _).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  count how many times a shifted colour S occurs inside a block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),
    color(S),
    N = #count { X : shifted(R,C,X), X = S }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  maximal frequency (M) inside a block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    M = #max { S : count(S,R,C,N), color(S) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  chosen colour – the *smallest* colour among those that achieve
%  the maximal frequency (ties broken by the smallest colour)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
chosen(R,C,V) :-
    block(R,C),
    count(V,R,C,N),
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    N = M,
    V = #min { W : count(W,R,C,Nw), color(W), Nw = M }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  output cell: one colour for each admissible block
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :- chosen(R,C,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one output per block position
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- block(R,C).
```

#const MAX = 18.   % max rows/cols

color(V) :- V = 0..9.

shift(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,Old), S = (Old + 2) mod 10.

% block existence
block(R,C) :- R = 0..(MAX-2)/2, C = 0..(MAX-2)/2,
    input(2*R, 2*C, _),
    input(2*R, 2*C+1, _),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C, _),
    input(2*R+1, 2*C+1, _).

% shift for block
shifted(R,C,V) :- block(R,C), shift(2*R, 2*C, V).

% count per colour in block
count(V,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),
    shift(R,C,V),
    N = #count { X : shift(R,C,X), X = V }.

% maximal count in block
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    M = #max { V : count(V,R,C,N), color(V) }.

% chosen colour: smallest colour among those with maximal count
chosen(R,C,V) :-
    block(R,C),
    count(V,R,C,N),
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    N = M,
    V = #min { W : count(W,R,C,Nw), color(W), Nw = M }.

output(R,C,V) :- chosen(R,C,V).

% Exactly one output per block
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- block(R,C), chosen(Col,R,C).

```asp
#const MAX = 31.

% colours
color(V) :- V = 0..9.

% shift of a single cell
shift(R,C,S) :- input(R,C,Old), S = (Old + 2) \ 10.

% a cell that participates in a 2x2 output block
block(R,C) :- input(2*R,   2*C,   _),
                input(2*R,   2*C+1, _),
                input(2*R+1, 2*C,   _),
                input(2*R+1, 2*C+1, _).

% shifted colour inside a block
shifted(R,C,S) :- block(R,C), shift(R,C,S).

% count how many times colour S appears in the block
count(S,R,C,N) :-
    block(R,C),
    shifted(R,C,S),
    N = #count { X : shifted(R,C,X), X = S }.

% maximal frequency in the block
maxcount(M,R,C) :-
    block(R,C),
    M = #max { V : count(V,R,C,N), color(V) }.

% the colour that will be written: smallest colour among those with maximal count
chosen(R,C,V) :-
    block(R,C),
    count(V,R,C,Nv),
    maxcount(M,R,C),
    Nv = M,
    V = #min { W : count(W,R,C,Nw), color(W), Nw = M }.

% output cell
output(R,C,V) :- chosen(R,C,V).

% exactly one colour for each admissible block
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- block(R,C), chosen(Col,R,C).

#show output(R,C,Colour).
```
